raw_user_request: Improve the dashboard UX for the sales pipeline app.
concrete_goal: Improve the sales pipeline dashboard UX so account executives can scan deal risk, filter opportunities, update stage status, and recover from empty or validation states without leaving the first screen.
success_criteria: Account executives can filter opportunities by stage, owner, and risk level from the dashboard.
The dashboard improves hierarchy for deal amount, next step, close date, stalled status, and stage movement.
Desktop and mobile layouts preserve readable cards, filters, and stage controls without overlap.
scope_in: User-facing dashboard UX improvements for the existing sales pipeline app.
Responsive layout, visual hierarchy, empty state, validation state, and accessibility basics for dashboard controls.
scope_out: Backend CRM integrations, authentication, billing, and production deployment.
assumptions: Local sample opportunity data is enough for the UX improvement pass.
implementation_constraints: Do not add paid services or external CRM API calls.

scenario_flows.name: Review at-risk sales opportunities
preconditions: The dashboard is loaded with sample opportunity records.
steps: Filter the dashboard to at-risk deals.
Update one opportunity stage and inspect the next-step panel.
scenario_flows.expected_result: Risk badges, deal amounts, stage status, and next steps remain visible and readable after the update.
failure_or_edge_cases: Empty filtered results show reset guidance.
Missing next-step text shows validation without moving controls.

verification_matrix.run: Open the local dashboard and report the concrete URL or static path.
verification_matrix.behavior_check: Check filtering, stage update, validation, empty state, responsive desktop/mobile, and keyboard focus behavior.

acceptance_evidence_plan: Desktop and mobile dashboard screenshots plus behavior, accessibility, and state coverage for the sales pipeline workflow.

delegation_plan_seed.objective: Improve the user-facing sales pipeline dashboard UX.
